methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Riverlane is a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $120M+ to date. The quantum computing sector includes three public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and eight private peers, including stage-adjacent companies like Infleqtion, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $120M total raised proxy, yielding $204M. Peer-median analysis is not supportable due to insufficient narrow peer clustering—while seven quantum computing companies exist in the broader comp universe, filtering by same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier produced fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide median analysis potentially misleading rather than anchoring.

The single applicable method supports a triangulated range of $173M to $235M, with $204M as the point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer multiples at Riverlane's specific stage and valuation tier, limiting confidence in stage-progression calibration. A more robust valuation would require either closer peer comparables or additional methods such as discounted cash flow analysis conditional on quantum computing commercialization timelines. The range should be treated as preliminary pending access to deeper comp financials or management guidance on near-term milestones.
